behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between source collectors and the summary-input assembler. A unit test of one function can miss wiring bugs where one source is dropped or mapped incorrectly.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with three test source adapters (metrics, issues, deploys) returning distinct sentinel payloads; assert the exact payload passed into summary-generation contains all three sections/keys.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on cross-module error propagation: fetch failure from one adapter must be converted into a section-level fallback note while the rest of assembly continues.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real report modules with one source adapter forced to raise a timeout/error and the other two returning data; assert process does not abort and output report includes `data unavailable` only for the failed section.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output quality (faithfulness to input values), which can vary across runs and is exactly what mocks would hide.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed structured input with known key values, call the real summary model, and evaluate whether required values/facts are represented correctly (with tolerance criteria) across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end boundary including real network/service behavior and inbox delivery semantics; mocks can pass while real SMTP/API auth, formatting, or delivery routing fails.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script against reachable test endpoints and a real test mailbox, then verify the recipient inbox receives the email with expected subject/body and report content.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is coordination between delivery failure handling, local persistence, and next-run retry logic. This can be caught without real scheduler/infra by running two orchestrated runs with shared local state.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: force email sender to fail and assert report is persisted to local fallback file/queue. Run 2: restore sender and execute next scheduled invocation path; assert queued report is retried and sent, then cleared from local retry state.
